edges.desc: serves as the characters' home base during the adventure
explore an island that has long been a battlefield in that conflict
each player chooses one character to play
Stormwreck Isle is a small island within the Sword Coast region.
The Characters / Party explore the Forgotten Realms.
The Characters / Party travel through the Sword Coast.
The Characters / Party are on Stormwreck Isle.
Runara serves as the leader of a tiny group of hermits and ascetics.
Runara resides on Stormwreck Isle, having established the cloister of Dragon's Rest there.
Runara established the cloister of Dragon's Rest as a safe haven.
Runara lives in human guise at the cloister entrance.
Runara established the cloister of Dragon's Rest, which is named Dragon's Rest.
Runara is a bronze dragon who killed a blue dragon, indicating rivalry with chromatic dragons.
The characters are involved in a conflict with Runara, who is a dragon and a key antagonist in the adventure.
Runara inhabits the Dragon's Rest location, which is her cloister.
Runara is associated with the Clifftop Observatory, where she killed a blue dragon.
The characters explore the Dragon's Rest location to learn about the island's problems.
The characters explore the Seagrow Caves, which are connected to the Elemental Plane of Fire.
The characters explore the Cursed Shipwreck, which is cursed and contains the bones of a gold dragon.
The characters explore the Clifftop Observatory, where a blue dragon's grandson has made his lair.
the characters arrive at the location marked on the map
receives information from the Dungeon Master about the game world
The characters engage the fire snake in combat.
The fire snake is immune to fire damage from the flaming sphere spell.
The characters may interact with Runara, who is a disguised bronze dragon leading the cloister of Dragon's Rest.
consults Appendix A to determine the properties of magic items
discover treasure including coins, gems, and art objects
The party encounters the Merrow Extortionist during their voyage to Stormwreck Isle.
The party is present on the island.
departed from the port city of Neverwinter
traveling towards the ominous island
located near the Sword Coast
The characters can use Charisma checks to reduce the amount of tribute demanded by the Merrow Extortionist.
The Merrow Extortionist describes the Scaled Queen as a two-headed merrow blessed by Demogorgon.
The characters can attack the Merrow Extortionist in combat
The Merrow Extortionist is guarded by the characters
The party is considering creative strategies to deal with the merrow.
The characters have dealt with the Merrow Extortionist, one way or another.
The characters are at Dragon's Rest
Dragon's Rest is associated with the dangers of Stormwreck Isle
The characters seek Runara's wisdom and assistance
the party is moving towards the cloister
arrive at Dragon's Rest and begin their exploration
the characters face a choice to fight or leave the zombies
zombies are present in the location
The party is near the shipwreck deck as they hear a ruckus behind them.
The party is near the shipwreck hull where the water is splashing.
The party is near the shipwreck hold, though not directly in it.
The party is near the shipwreck lower deck, though not directly in it.
The party is near the shipwreck harpy nest, though not directly in it.
The party hears a ruckus of splashing and a wet, gurgling moan from the shipwreck deck.
The party hears splashing and a wet, gurgling moan from the shipwreck hull.
The party hears splashing and a wet, gurgling moan from the shipwreck hold.
The party hears splashing and a wet, gurgling moan from the shipwreck lower deck.
The party hears splashing and a wet, gurgling moan from the shipwreck harpy nest.
the cleric's sacred flame cantrip deals radiant damage to zombies
comes to their rescue after zombies defeat the adventurers
wake up in the temple (area A5) of Dragon's Rest
the characters are at the cloister entrance
the characters are heading toward dragon's rest
path connects cloister entrance to dragon's rest
characters escape zombies at cloister entrance
path leads from cloister entrance to dragon's rest
Elder Runara welcomes the characters and offers them hospitality at Dragon's Rest
Myla sells equipment from the Basic Rules at Dragon's Rest
Myla resides at Dragon's Rest and sells equipment
The party arrives at the cloister entrance and is observed by the residents.
The party is present in the cloister, interacting with the kobolds and humans.
The party is present in the cloister
The party approaches the cloister
The party approaches Dragon's Rest
The party interacts with the elderly woman in the cloister
The party interacts with the elderly woman in Dragon's Rest
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest
Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest and guides the cloister's inhabitants.
Runara offers quests to the characters if they demonstrate care for the cloister's safety.
If the characters harm any of the residents of Dragon's Rest, Runara becomes hostile.
Runara guides the residents of the cloister in their contemplation and study.
Runara maintains a secret lair in a cave accessed by an undersea tunnel, but the other residents think she lives in the temple at the top of the island.
resides at Dragon's Rest
resides in Clifftop Observatory
attacked Myla, causing her wing injury
Runara helped heal Myla's injured wings
Myla's brothers follow the Blue Dragon Wyrmling
Rix witnessed a ship crash at the Cloister Entrance
tends the cloister's garden plots
frequently visits the sea caves to acquire heart cap mushrooms
made from heart cap mushrooms gathered from the sea caves
initially friendly but becomes hostile if questioned about his past
characters can engage Varnoth in conversation to shift her attitude to friendly
Varnoth resides at Dragon's Rest in her twilight years
Varnoth maintains the temple and other areas of the cloister
Varnoth witnessed a ship crash into the rocks to the north of the cloister
The party is exploring the clifftop observatory
characters ask Runara about the dragon statue
Runara tells the characters about Astalagan, the bronze dragon
The doorways lead into the cloister.
Tarak resides in a cell next to the westernmost cell.
Varnoth resides in a cell next to Tarak's cell.
Myla resides in a cluttered cell with junk and tools.
The westernmost cell is available to the characters if they don't mind sharing space.
uses the winch to haul goods up and down
the winch connects the cloister to the dragon's rest
Tarak is favored by the party for cooking
Myla visits the cloister library, though she is described as studious.
Myla is the only one described as studious, implying a possible rivalry with Runara.
Varnoth helps Runara maintain the temple
Runara is an inhabitant of Dragon's Rest
Varnoth is an inhabitant of Dragon's Rest
detect magic spell reveals a faint aura around the statue

explore Compass Rose Deck
the characters fight zombies on the island

Dragon's Rest is connected to the shipwreck lower deck
If the characters defeated the zombies when they first arrived on the island
Tarak asks the party to visit the caves, find out what's wrong with the myconids, and bring him back some heart cap mushrooms.
Tarak gives the party two Potion of Healing.
Tarak is eager to reestablish contact with the myconids of the sea caves.
The fungal octopus is a guardian of the myconids in the caves.
The party is tasked with visiting the seagrow caves to investigate the myconids' situation.
The characters are asked by Varnoth and Rix about the shipwreck
The characters ask Runara about the shipwreck
Runara suggests the answer is on the Compass Rose wreck

Runara knows Varnoth and encourages the paladin to talk to him
paladin is encouraged to spend time in the temple of Bahamut (implied to be in Runara's Study)
Runara asks the paladin if they have learned anything about combating corruption
The rogue interrogates Tarak about the lost fortune and his past with the Gilded Gallows.
The party helps Runara with other problems on the island
Runara promises to direct the wizard to the observatory after the party helps with other problems
Runara was at the cloister when Aidron left
Runara suspects evil has arisen at the observatory ruins
The characters need access to the observatory ruins to proceed
Runara gives the moonstone key to the characters
changes form from human to dragon
keeps an eye on them and can rescue them if things go badly for them on the island
awaken in the temple (area A5) if unconscious
Runara keeps an eye on them in her study
the characters travel between locations on the island
the characters travel between locations on the island
the characters travel between locations on the island
the fume drakes attack the characters if they attempt to gather mushrooms or enter the hot spring
the fume drakes are hidden in the water and only visible upon a successful Wisdom (Perception) check
A character in the party examines the mushrooms lining the spring and identifies them as wind spores.
Wind spore mushrooms contain wind spores that provide oxygen when squeezed.
Each wind spore is worth 30 gp.
At any given time, 2d4 wind spores are ready to be harvested.
The party encounters an owlbear that is hostile toward them but aims to drive them away rather than kill them.
The owlbear is hostile toward the characters, viewing them as intruders in its territory.
The owlbear considers the area it inhabits as its territory and will become hostile again if forced to leave.
The owlbear was originally part of a performing troupe that crashed on the northern rocks of the island.
The owlbear has a small wooden whistle around its neck that can be used to command it.
Characters within 5 feet of the owlbear can attempt to grab the whistle with a Strength check.
A character can use the whistle to make a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check to calm the owlbear.
On a successful check, the owlbear becomes friendly toward the whistle holder and indifferent toward the other characters.
ambushes the characters while hiding in the rocks and light foliage
both kobold types are present in the ambush
the kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara
the winged kobold also rejects the peaceful teachings of Runara
characters investigate Sharruth's tomb
characters may access subterranean passages if they search
seagrow caves are beneath stormwreck isle
the characters come here before going to the wreck of Compass Rose
the characters are going to the wreck of Compass Rose next
the characters might complete chapter 3 before coming here, making them 2nd level
Tarak trades with the myconids in the Seagrow Caves
Tarak trades with the cloister for food scraps
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids in the Seagrow Caves
Seagrow Caves are located on Stormwreck Isle
Seagrow Caves trade with Dragon's Rest
Seagrow Caves trade with the Cloister
Seagrow Caves are connected to the Compass Rose Deck
Tarak trades with Sinensa
Sinensa inhabits the Seagrow Caves
Seagrow Caves trade with Tarak
The lesser restoration spell ends the poisoning effect caused by toxic fumes in the caves.
the characters can take the rowboat from Dragon's Rest to Seagrow Caves

rowboat route from Dragon's Rest to Seagrow Caves

coastal route from Seagrow Caves to Dragon's Rest

the party is located near the cave mouth
The characters are exploring the Seagrow Caves.
The party is present in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconids inhabit the Seagrow Caves.
Myconids release distress spores to alert each other within 240 feet.
Myconids in areas B2 and B3 move to area B4 upon detecting distress spores.
the spore servant octopus attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, regardless of the state of the tides
the spore servant octopus is a guardian created by the myconid leader to keep outsiders away
If the characters are 2nd level, two Stirge clinging to the tunnel ceiling are disturbed if fighting breaks out in this area and join the battle.
The stirges ignore the spore servant.
The party is in the Seagrow Caves where Myconid Sprouts are present.
The party is in the Seagrow Caves where Myconid Adults are present.
The party is threatened by Violet Fungus in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Sprouts and Adults coexist in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Sprouts coexist with Violet Fungus in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Adults coexist with Violet Fungus in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Sprouts and Adults work together in the Seagrow Caves.
Myconid Sprouts are unaware of the Violet Fungus threat.
Myconid Adults are unaware of the Violet Fungus threat.
encounters a sickly looking mushroom that stirs to life
The characters use ranged attacks to kill the violet fungi.
The violet fungi pose a danger to the characters.
Some violet fungi remain motionless until characters move close to them.
Characters scan the fungus farm, which causes the fungi to appear more dangerous.
The Myconid Sprout avoid the characters and the violet fungi.
If the Myconid Adult become aware of danger through the noise of combat (or the sprouts' Distress Spores), they move as quickly as they can to protect the sprouts.

If the characters defeat the violet fungi, the myconids' attitude improves to indifferent, and they are willing to speak to the characters (using their Rapport Spores). The adults agree to accompany the characters and vouch for them with the rest of the myconid colony, improving the other myconids' attitude to indifferent as well.
Tarak can make each heart cap mushroom into a potion of healing
Tarak can make each heart cap mushroom into a potion of healing
If the characters are 2nd level, two Violet Fungus are added to this encounter.
flee toward area B4 when characters enter the chamber
six stirges attack characters who enter the chamber
stirge nest provides distraction for sprouts to escape
watching the three small mushroom-folk working in the filth
the bulbous object is clinging to the wall and ceiling in the southwest corner
six myconid adults are present in the seagrow caves
four myconid adults are in a dream-like trance called a meld
two myconid adults guard the caves and respond with violence to threats
myconid adults are ill and fatigued from the blight and may attack intruders not escorted by other myconids
Two Myconid Adults tend to and protect the unconscious leader Sinensa
The Myconid Adults zealously defend their leader from intruders
The Myconid Adults collect spores from a glowing red fungus resembling a human brain
The Myconid Adults puff spores around the leader's head to keep them alive
Tarak uses the ruby morel to create an elixir of health, which is given to the characters
Tarak uses the ruby morel to make the elixir of health
The Fume Drake lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in the Seagrow Caves.
the fire snake is located within the seagrow caves
The characters encounter fume drakes when they first arrive in the room.
The fire snake and fume drakes coexist in the same environment, with the fire snake hatching from its egg while the fume drakes emerge from the crystals.
The characters encounter the fire snake as it hatches from its egg.
characters destroy the orange crystal to improve myconids' attitude
gives ruby morel and permission to keep treasure

gives elixir of health in gratitude

The party is located at the Shipwreck Shoal after the wreck.
Many Undead creatures (like Ghoul) worship him or seek to bargain with him in exchange for some fragment of his power over undeath.

The party is present at the shipwreck harpy nest.
The harpy speaks Common and can be reasoned with by the party.
If the characters reduce the harpy to fewer than half its hit points, it might flee.
If the characters claimed the treasure from the crow's nest while the harpy was absent, it might agree to leave in exchange for the return of its treasure.
If the characters are 2nd level and two harpies are present, they can play on the distrust between the two harpies and might convince them to part ways and leave the area.
The characters encounter harpies in the shipwreck harpy nest.
The characters interact with harpies in the shipwreck harpy nest.
If the characters are 2nd level, a second harpy joins the encounter.
Runara is associated with the clifftop cemetery where Brastos is laid to rest.
